topic.name: Federal Appropriations and Authorizations
topic.description: This cluster discusses various federal appropriations and authorizations for programs and projects, including those for veterans, law enforcement, and the arts.

Document 1: annual authorization of appropriations for Department of Veterans Affairs comprehensive service programs for homeless veterans
Document 2: increase by at least 50 for each of FY2008-FY2012 the number of full-time Deputy U.S. Marshals that investigate criminal immigration matters
Authorizes appropriations
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations for FY2008-FY2012 to the Board for maintenance, repair, and security
Authorizes appropriations for such system

topic.name: Public Health Initiatives
topic.description: Legislative acts aimed at improving health outcomes and reducing disease prevalence among specific populations.

Document 1: Lung Cancer Mortality Reduction Act of 2008
comprehensive program to achieve a 50% reduction in the mortality rate of lung cancer
Document 2: Minority Diabetes Initiative Act
treatment for diabetes in minority communities
Document 3: National Neurological Diseases Surveillance System Act of 2011
track the epidemiology of neurological diseases

topic.name: Tax Legislation for Specific Groups
topic.description: This topic encompasses various tax-related legislative acts aimed at providing financial benefits or simplifications for specific demographics, including children, seniors, and military personnel.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to Coverdale education savings accounts
allow distributions from such accounts for the purchase of athletic equipment at elementary and secondary schools.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to make available a new federal income tax Form 1040SR
Allows the form to be used even if income includes social security benefits.
Document 3: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to military personnel
make permanent the taxpayer election to treat combat zone compensation as earned income for purposes of the earned income tax credit.

topic.name: Prescription Drug Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving the affordability, accessibility, and innovation of prescription drugs.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a nonrefundable tax credit for 80 percent of the amount paid for a prescribed drug
Allows a person who meets applicable legal requirements to be an importer of prescription drugs
Document 2: Establishes procedures under which the term of an unexpired patent claiming a method of using a biological product shall be extended for five years
Defines 'independent innovator' as a person or entity that obtains a method of use patent for a biological product
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to expand the definition of 'first applicant'
Requires such applicant to submit a substantially complete application that contains and lawfully maintains a certification for such drug

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Security Assistance
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures and acts aimed at addressing international security, child protection, and foreign assistance related to countries facing significant challenges.

Document 1: Child Protection Compact Act of 2011
trafficking in children
Document 2: Prohibits U.S. government funds from being used to provide non-security assistance to Mexico
securing Mexico's border with the United States
Document 3: Afghan Responsibility for Afghan Security Act
transition of security responsibilities in Afghanistan

topic.name: Water Quality and Protection
topic.description: Legislation focused on ensuring the safety and quality of drinking water and managing risks associated with contaminants and nonnative species.

Document 1: amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to direct the Environmental Protection Agency to develop and submit to Congress a strategic plan for assessing and managing risks associated with algal toxins in drinking water
evaluate the risk to human health from drinking water contaminated with algal toxins
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to promulgate regulations establishing a process for assessing the risk of all nonnative wildlife species proposed for importation into the United States

Sets forth factors that must be considered at minimum, including the identity of the organism to the species level, the geographic source, and the likelihood of spread and harm to groups of species or habitats.
Document 3: calls for the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to prioritize the types of technical assistance to enable small public water systems to comply with national primary drinking water regulations
authorize appropriations to the Administrator of EPA for FY2014-FY2019 for such assistance

topic.name: U.S. Sanctions Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on U.S. legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions on specific countries and entities to prevent activities related to proliferation and other national security concerns.

Document 3: requires that all U.S.-imposed sanctions with respect to Belarus remain in effect until the President certifies to Congress that the government of Belarus has met specified conditions
The President shall include the Joint Stock Company Belaruskali and the Joint Stock Company Belarusian Potash Company on the list of specially designated nationals and blocked persons.

topic.name: Education Policy Reforms
topic.description: Legislative changes aimed at modifying funding, assessment, and community engagement in K-12 education.

Document 1: Repeals specified provisions of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965.
Lists the repealed provisions as those pertaining to various educational programs.
Document 2: Amends subpart 1 (Accountability) of part A of title VI of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965.
Requires each state to use such grant to develop and administer assessments aligned with academic content standards.
Document 3: Amends part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize local educational agencies to use school improvement funds.
Defines a 'community school' as a public elementary or secondary school that partners with community-based entities.

topic.name: Health Insurance Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the review and correction of health insurance premium rates to protect consumers.

Document 1: federal requirements that the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) review unreasonable premium increases
corrective actions to ensure that any excessive, unjustified, or unfairly discriminatory rates are corrected
Document 2: federal provisions requiring the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to review unreasonable premium increases
corrective actions to ensure that any excessive, unjustified, or unfairly discriminatory rates are corrected

topic.name: Fuel Regulations and Emissions
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulations related to fuel blends, emissions reduction programs, and tax equalization for alternative fuels.

Document 1: Authorizes the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to allow the introduction into commerce of a mid-level ethanol blend
EPA's Science Advisory Board submits to the Administrator, and the Administrator provides for public notice and comment
Document 2: This bill amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to reauthorize through FY2021 a diesel emissions reduction program
The program authorizes the Environmental Protection Agency to provide grants, rebates, or loans for reducing diesel emissions
Document 3: This bill equalizes the excise tax on liquefied petroleum gas and liquefied natural gas
establishing a rate of 18.3 cents per energy equivalent of a gallon of gasoline for liquefied petroleum gas

topic.name: Social Security and Benefits Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the disbursement and taxation of benefits under various social security and veterans programs.

Document 1: disburse a one-time payment equal to 60% of the total annual amount of their benefits under Social Security, SSI, railroad retirement benefits program, and veterans disability compensation
if no cost-of-living adjustment is payable in 2011.
Document 2: exempt from social security taxes remuneration paid to any elected or appointed member of any general governing board
if such remuneration is not more than $1,300 and is paid for service performed on or after January 1, 2008.
Document 3: disburse a payment equal to 3.9% of the average amount of annual benefits
who are entitled to a specified benefit under title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) of the Social Security Act

topic.name: Data Protection and Fraud Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing data protection measures and preventing fraud in various sectors, particularly healthcare and personal information management.

Document 1: Amends title XI of the Social Security Act with respect to standards for financial and administrative transactions
Prohibits the Secretary from adopting a new or modified code set unless the Secretary assesses its impact on fraud prevention
Document 2: Prohibits data brokers from obtaining or causing to be disclosed personal information by making a false, fictitious, or fraudulent statement
Requires data brokers to establish procedures to ensure the accuracy of the personal information they collect

topic.name: Federal Judicial Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the federal judicial system addressing bail bond regulations, judgeship allocations, and emergency court procedures.

Document 1: Bail Bond Fairness Act of 2007
prohibit a judicial officer from declaring forfeited a bail bond for violation of specified collateral release conditions
Document 2: Amends the federal judicial code to provide for an additional judgeship for the western district of Michigan
increasing the number of judgeships from four to five
Document 3: Responsive Government Act of 2008
authorize the chief judge of a district or court of appeals to delay, toll, or otherwise grant relief from time deadlines

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States related to the temporary suspension or reduction of duties on specific goods.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on a certain ion exchange resin.
temporary suspension of duty
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on 2-Oxepanone, polymer with 2,2-dimethyl-1,3-propanediol.
suspend temporarily the duty
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to temporarily extend the reduction of duty on pepperoncini, prepared or preserved by vinegar.
temporarily extend the reduction of duty

topic.name: Children's Mental Health Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving access to mental health services and care for children and adolescents.

Document 1: Ensuring Children’s Access to Specialty Care Act of 2015
pediatric subspecialists, including psychiatrists, are included in behavioral and mental health professionals.
Document 2: Garrett Lee Smith Memorial Act Reauthorization of 2010
expands the program's focus from youth suicides to suicides among all ages, particularly among groups that are at high risk for suicide.
Document 3: Mental Illness Chronic Care Improvement Act of 2009
development, testing, evaluation, and implementation of severe mental illness chronic care improvement demonstration programs.

topic.name: National Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislation related to national security, intelligence gathering, and surveillance in the United States.

Document 1: Amends provisions of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA)
Revises requirements for applications for access to business records in counterterrorism investigations.
Document 2: Requires the National Security Agency (NSA) to have access to business records
Amends the USA PATRIOT Improvement and Reauthorization Act of 2005.

topic.name: Early Childhood Education and Care
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving access to and the quality of early childhood education and care for young children, particularly those from low-income families.

Document 1: authorizes the Secretary of Education to award competitive matching grants to states for full day voluntary prekindergarten programs
ensure that, within two years of grant receipt, each classroom is taught by a teacher who has at least a baccalaureate degree in early childhood education
Document 2: expand access to high-quality child care for infants and toddlers from low-income families
increase the quality of such care for infants and toddlers who do receive child care funded through the CCD Fund
Document 3: creates a Pediatric Early Literacy program under which the Secretary of Education awards matching grants to nonprofit organizations
health care providers encourage parents to read aloud to their children

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Resource Extraction
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management of federal lands, including protections, land exchanges, and regulations concerning the extraction of energy and mineral resources.

Document 1: Withdraws the approximately 982,550 acres of the Tusayan Ranger District in Arizona
Subjects such withdrawal to valid, existing rights.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to issue regulations to establish a specified graduated annual production incentive fee
Prohibits the Secretary from issuing new oil or natural gas production leases in the Gulf of Mexico.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey certain federal land in Pinal County, Arizona to Resolution Copper
Requires preparation of an environmental review document to be used as the basis for all decisions under federal law.

topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing employment opportunities and support for individuals with disabilities.

expanding individual integrated employment and reducing subminimum wage work

topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at reforming criminal justice policies, particularly concerning sex offender management and drug-related offenses.

Document 1: reauthorize through FY2021 the Sex Offender Management Assistance program
extend the statute of limitations for a minor victim of a federal sex offense to file a civil action to 10 years
Document 2: establish criminal penalties of a fine, imprisonment for not more than 20 years, or both for trafficking, or attempting to traffic, in counterfeit drugs

Document 1: prohibits a sanctuary jurisdiction from receiving grants
increases maximum prison term for an alien who reenters after being denied admission
Document 2: requires federal, state, and local governments to directly operate adult prisons
prohibits family detention and requires alternatives to detention programs

topic.name: Tribal Water Rights and Management
topic.description: Documents discussing the legal frameworks and agreements related to water rights and management for Native American tribes in the United States.

Document 1: Approves, ratifies, and confirms the Settlement Agreement... relating to the water rights of such Tribe.
Establishes in the Treasury: (1) the San Jacinto Basin Restoration Fund... for accommodating supplemental imported water deliveries.
Document 2: Amends the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 to modify provisions authorizing the Cherokee Nation of Oklahoma to design and construct hydroelectric generating facilities...
Requires such Nation to obtain any permit required by federal or state law before the date on which construction begins on such facilities.
Document 3: Deems requirements of the Endangered Species Act of 1973 relating to operations of the Central Valley Project... to be satisfied...
Authorize the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Commerce ensure that...

topic.name: Food Safety Regulations
topic.description: Legislation focused on the safety and labeling of food products to protect public health.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prohibit the Commissioner of the Food and Drug Administration from using federal funds to amend specified regulations
include dry ultra-filtered milk, milk protein concentrate, or casein in the definition of 'milk' or 'nonfat milk'
Document 2: Amends the Federal Meat Inspection Act and the Poultry Products Inspection Act
direct a person who believes a meat or poultry product that such person handled is adulterated or misbranded to notify the Secretary of Agriculture
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to provide that a food shall be deemed to be misbranded
contains any spice, flavoring, or coloring derived from meat, poultry, any other animal product unless its labeling bears a statement

topic.name: Immigration Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. immigration laws and acts that provide pathways for specific groups of individuals to obtain legal status.

Document 1: makes 3,000 immigrant visas available to individuals who were born in Tibet
available to the sons, daughters, grandsons, or granddaughters of such individuals.
Document 2: authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security to cancel the removal of, and adjust to conditional permanent resident status
sets forth the conditions for conditional permanent resident status

topic.name: Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating and improving the operations and accessibility of the U.S. Postal Service.

Document 1: Requires the head of each executive agency to establish safeguards and internal controls for purchase cards, convenience checks, and travel cards.
Requires each agency Inspector General to periodically conduct risk assessments of agency purchase and travel card programs.
Document 2: Prohibits the U.S. Postal Service from closing, consolidating, or suspending the operations of more than 5% of postal facilities.
Requires the USPS to conduct nonbinding surveys on proposed post office closings or consolidations.
Document 3: Prohibits the U.S. Postal Service from requiring centralized delivery for certain residential housing units.
The USPS must establish curbside delivery for such units.

topic.name: Healthcare and Civil Rights Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving healthcare access and protecting civil rights related to health and discrimination based on sexual orientation, gender identity, and religion.

Document 1: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to allow a higher payment to states for routine HIV/AIDS and STI screening services
requires coverage of low-income HIV/AIDS infected individuals.
Document 2: Amends the Civil Rights Act of 1964 to include sex, sexual orientation, and gender identity among the prohibited categories of discrimination
Prohibits 'establishment' from being construed to be limited to a physical facility or place.
Document 3: prohibit any person, on the ground of religion, from being excluded from participation in, being denied the benefits of, or being subjected to discrimination under an educational program
States rules of construction concerning religious affiliation of educational institutions.

topic.name: Land Exchange and Rights-of-Way
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative actions regarding the exchange and management of land rights involving federal and nonfederal lands.

Document 1: authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to accept the quitclaim, disclaimer, and relinquishment by the Manitou and Pikes Peak Railway Company of a specified right-of-way
adjacent to Pike National Forest
Document 2: directs the Department of Agriculture (USDA) to accept the offer and convey to Yavapai County 80 acres of specified federal land
lands acquired by the USDA under this bill shall become part of the Coconino National Forest
Document 3: authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to convey to the city certain federal land in exchange for such land
total of approximately 1,680 acres to the Secretary

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses legislative measures aimed at the conservation and management of wildlife, specifically polar bears and Gulf of Mexico red snapper.

Document 1: Restoration of the U.S.-Russia Polar Bear Conservation Fund Act of 2013
Amends the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972 to allow the importation of polar bear parts taken legally in sport hunts in Canada.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to issue a permit for the importation of polar bear parts
Polar bears were listed as a threatened species by the Department of the Interior on May 14, 2008.
Document 3: Gulf of Mexico Red Snapper Conservation Act of 2013

Directs the Gulf States Marine Fisheries Commission to prepare, adopt, and submit to the Secretary of Commerce a fishery management plan providing for the conservation and management of Gulf of Mexico red snapper.

topic.name: Legal Rights and Protections
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legal acts aimed at protecting individual rights and providing avenues for redress in cases of injustice or discrimination.

Document 1: Grants U.S. district courts original jurisdiction over any civil action for damages for personal injury or death that arose from the deportation of persons to Nazi concentration camps.
no such action shall be barred because a statute of limitations has expired.
Document 2: Declares that a person not prohibited under the Brady Handgun Violence Prevention Act from receiving a firearm shall have the right to obtain firearms for security.
Authorizes persons whose rights under this Act have been violated to bring an action in U.S. district court against the United States, any state, or any person for damages.
Document 3: Amends the Civil Rights Act of 1964 to declare that an unlawful employment practice occurs when a discriminatory compensation decision or other practice is adopted.
Allows liability to accrue, and allows an aggrieved person to obtain relief, including recovery of back pay, for up to two years preceding the filing of the charge.

topic.name: Education and Economic Development for Indigenous Peoples

qualified nursing or medical school
